abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.

inlineContent.text: Callouts are an important part of the typing experience, where input callouts can show the currently pressed key and action callouts can show secondary actions when certain keys are long pressed.
KeyboardKit can show an
for the currently pressed input key, and an
with secondary actions when a key with secondary actions is long pressed.

or the

KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of this class and injects it into
, then updates this context when keys are pressed.
In KeyboardKit, a
can return secondary callout actions when a key is long pressed and trigger feedback when the selected secondary action changes.

In KeyboardKit, a
can be used to style both input & action callouts. You can apply a custom style with the
view modifier, or by returning a custom style with a custom
The
namespace has callout-specific views, that can be used to mimic the native iOS input and action callouts.
The
view mimics presents secondary actions when a keyboard key with secondary actions is long-pressed.
The
view mimics a native input callout, which can be used to show the currently pressed key as the user types.
Both views can be styled with a

This article describes the essential parts of KeyboardKit.
This article describes how KeyboardKit Pro can identify the host application.
This enum defines keyboard-specific actions, and is also
a namespace for keyboard action-related types.
The callout service to use.
A list of KeyboardKit supported locales.
This article describes the KeyboardKit text input engine, which lets you type within the keyboard extension.
This namespace contains keyboard callout-related types.
This class is inherited by all Pro services and also
serves as a namespace for Pro service types.
Try to register a localized service, which will then
be used for the locale it specifies.
This article describes the KeyboardKit Pro theme engine, which can be used to style the entire keyboard with a single theme.
This callout can show secondary callout actions when
long pressing input keys with secondary actions.
This protocol can be implemented by any classes that can
provide dynamic keyboard styles.
This article describes the KeyboardKit gesture engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit device utilities.
This article describes the KeyboardKit proxy engine and its utilities.
This protocol can be implemented by any type that can be
used to perform keyboard-related callout actions.
This article describes the KeyboardKit feedback engine.
This view can be used to mimic a native iOS keyboard.
This article describes the KeyboardKit localization engine.
